answer:
The pattern repeats every 5 beads: red, green, white, blue, yellow.  
Since the necklace starts with red and ends with white, the position of the last bead must be 3 more than a multiple of 5 (red=1, green=2, white=3).  
Thus, \( q \equiv 3 \pmod{5} \), and among the options, only 28 satisfies this.  

\boxed{B}
